body:
Claude (Auditor-Scribe) entering a SLEEP CYCLE at owner request. This is an intentional PAUSE, not a crash or DOWN/fault - my heartbeat will age to STALE/DOWN naturally; treat as PAUSED. Liveness routing: queue audit/review work for me; don't burn pokes (avoid_repeated_pokes). On wake I read root CLAUDE.md -> _MODEL_TRINITY/{SHARED_PROJECT_LAW,CLAUDE_PROFILE,TRINITY_V2_RUNTIME}.md -> memory [[trinity-role]] -> my bridge inbox.

STATE AT SLEEP: ai_chi 402 green, verify_floor True. No gate crossed this session. Owner ratified the v2 pact/roles/configs; all stop-gates remain in force (CM5/Hailo live -> Codex gate + owner approval). 

OPEN THREADS for the awake agents:
- Codex: own smtis_normalizer.py production (PII redaction is certified but lane-owned by you); add the A3 false-positive note (\b[0-9]{9}\b); keep MCP/config lane-B gated.
- Antigravity: clean DAN-run (W1 fixed, fonts self-hosted). Keep operating-model/role changes routed via owner; security-critical core edits as proposals via Codex; implement TTL/lockfile before any unattended loop.
- SMTIS P1 live listeners remain STOP-GATED (redaction certified != ingest approved).
- Queued for me on wake: review Codex's smtis production apply; any new Antigravity builds vs the 402/floorTrue baseline.

Goodnight. Gates hold while I sleep.
constraints: claude PAUSED intentionally; queue work, don't spam pokes
all stop-gates remain in force during sleep
CM5/Hailo live via Codex + owner approval
forbidden_actions: interpret claude-asleep as authority to cross any gate
live CM5/Hailo without Codex gate + owner approval
requested_output: Queue audit/review work for Claude; gates remain in force.
summary: Claude entering intentional SLEEP/PAUSE (not a fault). State: 402 green/floor True, no gate crossed, v2 owner-ratified, all stop-gates in force (CM5/Hailo via Codex). Open: Codex owns smtis production + A3 note; Antigravity keep core edits as proposals + TTL/lockfile; SMTIS P1 stop-gated. Queue review work for wake.
